sevenDayForecast.headline: Tropical Cyclone Fina to move towards the Top End coast during Thursday.
points: Tropical Cyclone Advices and Forecast Track Maps are being issued for Tropical Cyclone Fina (02U) and should be referred to for more detailed information
Tropical Cyclone Fina, category 2 lies in the Arafura Sea off the north coast of the Top End and is moving slowly to the east.
Environmental conditions are mostly favourable and Fina may develop further over the coming days.

Fina is forecast to move slowly to the east before taking a track to the south during Thursday towards the northern Top End coast. It is expected to move to the southwest from Friday, impacting the northwest Top End.
On Sunday and early week the system may continue on a southwest track through the Timor Sea. Conditions become less favourable from Monday, and Fina should start to weaken.

trackForecast.headline: Tropical Cyclone Fina remains slow moving and is still expected to move towards the north Top End coast during Thursday.
trackForecast.discussion:
Tropical Cyclone Fina remains a Category 2 system and is slow moving. Recent microwave and scatterometry satellite images has improved the confidence in position and the small extent of gales.¿ 

Environmental conditions are mostly favourable for development and the system may intensify further over the coming days.  Being a small system, Fina may fluctuate in intensity and be quick to intensify but also weaken quickly too. The intensity is capped at 60kn (category 2)  as there is uncertainty in the role of potentially increasing northerly shear from Friday.  

Models are consistent for Fina to move slowly generally to the east before turning to the south during Thursday, then southwest towards the NT's northwest coast on Friday and Saturday. There is still considerable spread in the degree of the southwest turning and coastal impact location at this stage. From Sunday the system is expected to move southwest into the Timor Sea, where it may start to weaken early next week.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: The upper bound scenario reflects the uncertainty shown in the model guidance and the possibility that the system could intensify at a more rapid rate especially given its small size as well as remain over water for the entire forecast period.
